reasoning: The base rate of nuclear testing by the US, Russia, and China is effectively zero over the past 30 years due to the CTBT moratorium. Despite political rhetoric in late 2025 about potentially resuming testing, and February 2026 U.S. declassified allegations that China conducted a small secret test in 2020, there have been no public reports of any actual nuclear detonation by these three countries between November 2025 and March 15, 2026. Nuclear explosive tests require extensive preparation that is almost always detected by satellite imagery or seismic monitors beforehand, yet there is currently no evidence of an imminent test. Given that the resolution date is April 1, 2026 (only 16 days away) and the Metaculus community was at 0.00% as of March 6, the likelihood of a detonation occurring and being reported before the deadline is exceptionally low.
